1.title: Understanding the Project
1.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you'll organize a messy folder into a structured workspace using Linux terminal commands. You'll create directories, move files, and verify your changes using commands like `mkdir`, `mv`, `ls`, and `pwd`. This project will help you practice managing files and directories efficiently in a Linux environment.

2.title: Creating Directories
2.bodyMarkdown: To start organizing, you'll need to create directories for different types of files. Use the `mkdir` command to create new directories. For example, `mkdir notes` will create a directory named 'notes'. If you need to create nested directories, use `mkdir -p`. For instance, `mkdir -p school/homework` creates both 'school' and 'homework' directories if they don't exist.

3.title: Moving Files
3.bodyMarkdown: Once directories are created, you can move files into them using the `mv` command. For example, `mv document.txt notes/` moves 'document.txt' into the 'notes' directory. This command helps in organizing files into their respective folders.
